g.data-narration: Welcome to understanding the CPU core components: ALU performs calculations while the Control Unit directs operations.
g.text: The ALU and Control Unit: The Core of the CPU

g.data-narration: The von Neumann architecture has five main components: CPU, Memory, I/O devices, and Buses connecting them all.
Inside the CPU, the ALU handles math operations while the Control Unit coordinates all activities through registers.
The CPU repeats the fetch-decode-execute cycle billions of times per second to run programs.
g.text: The Instruction Cycle: Fetch, Decode, Execute

g.data-narration: Registers are small, fast memory locations inside the CPU that hold data being processed immediately.
In the Fetch stage, the PC holds the instruction address, which is read from memory into the IR register.
In the Decode stage, the Control Unit examines the instruction to understand the opcode and operands.
The ALU performs four main types of operations: arithmetic, logical, shift, and comparison operations.
g.text: ALU Operations: What the ALU Can Do

g.data-narration: Status flags are set by the ALU to report results: Zero, Carry, Overflow, and Sign conditions.
During Execute, the ALU performs the operation. Here we trace how ADD R1, R2 moves data through the CPU.
g.text: EXECUTE Stage: ADD R1, R2 Example

g.data-narration: The Control Unit acts as the conductor, generating timing and control signals to coordinate all CPU components.
g.text: 2. Generate control signals to coordinate components
3. Step through sequence of control states
4. Manage data flow via internal bus

g.data-narration: There are two ways to implement control: hardwired (fast, inflexible) or microprogrammed (flexible, slower).
g.text: Uses state machine of logic gates. Very fast but inflexible.
Uses microcode in control store. Flexible and easier to modify.
Slower than hardwired due to extra interpretation.

g.data-narration: Hardwired control excels in speed for simple instruction sets, while microprogrammed offers easier design and modification.
g.text: Microprogrammed: Speed ✓ | Flexibility ✓✓✓ | Easier to modify

g.data-narration: This diagram shows complete data flow: operands move from registers through ALU to accumulator, controlled by CU signals.
Remember: ALU does the math operations, while CU directs the flow. The fetch-decode-execute cycle runs continuously.
For exams: know the fetch-decode-execute cycle, differentiate ALU/CU functions, memorize status flags, and practice drawing data flow diagrams.
In summary, the CPU executes programs through the fetch-decode-execute cycle, with the ALU performing operations and the CU orchestrating everything.
g.text: Summary: The Core of the CPU
